title: icacorea standleyana
intro: Icacorea standleyana, also known as the 'Mortiño' tree, is a plant species native to Central and South America, belonging to the family Sapindaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including the relief of pain, reduced inflammation, and improved wound healing, making it a valuable resource for treating various health conditions. Icacorea standleyana can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion for medicinal use, although its safety and efficacy should be further studied and confirmed.
